title: execute_csharp: live Unity object descriptor (no infinite loop)

steps.title: Prerequisites
steps.body: Bridge running. Goal: confirm the depth-limited reflective serializer returns a compact descriptor for a live GameObject/Component instead of reflecting into the cyclic GameObject↔Component graph (which previously infinite-looped or threw).

steps.title: Return a live Transform from execute_csharp

payload.code: var go = new GameObject("Probe"); return go.transform;

steps.title: Expected outcomes
items: mutation.output is a compact descriptor object, e.g. {"$type":"UnityEngine.Transform", "name":"Probe", "instanceId":<int>, ...}
No reflection recurses into the GameObject↔Component graph (no hang, no stack overflow)
If include_properties is on, computed properties appear but the graph stays bounded

reset.note: execute_csharp creates a transient GameObject in the open scene; it is not persisted to disk and is not tracked by the fs manifest. If a leftover "Probe" object clutters the scene, delete it manually in the Editor (it is not part of the disposable fixture tree).
